grants.title: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - incubators apply quarterly
grants.description: Government-backed seed funding disbursed through DPIIT-recognised incubators. Covers proof of concept (up to Rs.20L as grant) and prototype development and market entry (up to Rs.50L as soft loan).
grants.eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startup incorporated not more than 2 years ago. Must apply through a supported incubator - you don't approach DPIIT directly. Cannot be a subsidiary or JV of another company. Cannot have received more than Rs.10L of funding from any source previously.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.20L as a grant for proof of concept, prototype development, and product trials
Up to Rs.50L as a soft loan (convertible debenture) for market entry and commercialisation
Mentorship and incubation support from the partner incubator
Access to DPIIT's startup network and government procurement opportunities
grants.howToApply: Find a DPIIT-recognised incubator near you from the official portal. Apply to get incubated first - the incubator then applies to SISFS on your behalf. Selections happen quarterly.

grants.title: Atal New India Challenge (ANIC)
grants.deadlineLabel: Sector-wise open calls - check portal for active cohorts
grants.description: Grant-based innovation challenge for startups and MSMEs building technology solutions to national problems in agriculture, water, waste management, health, and clean energy. Disbursed in milestones after independent validation.
grants.eligibility: Indian startups or MSMEs registered under Companies Act or MSME Act. Must have a working prototype addressing the specific challenge theme. Individual innovators can apply but need institutional affiliation.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.1 Cr non-dilutive grant disbursed across 3-4 milestones
Technical mentorship from domain experts and IIT/IIM faculty
Pilot opportunities with government departments and PSUs
Market access support and introduction to procurement channels
Recognition and media visibility through NITI Aayog network
grants.howToApply: Monitor the AIM portal for active challenge themes. Applications are online through the AIM website. Shortlisted teams go through a technical evaluation and pitch. Selected startups sign a grant agreement and milestones are defined upfront.

grants.title: Biotechnology Ignition Grant (BIG)
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - two open windows per year (usually Jan and Jul)
grants.description: India's flagship biotech grant for early-stage innovations in healthcare, agriculture biotech, and industrial biotech. One of the few grants that funds idea-to-prototype stage without requiring a registered company.
grants.eligibility: Individual innovators, scientists, startups, or research groups working on a biotech innovation. Must have a host institution (university, hospital, incubator, or biotech company). Indian nationals or organisations only.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.50L as a non-dilutive grant over 18 months
Access to BIRAC's network of 90+ incubators across India
IP support - BIRAC helps with patent filing costs
Regulatory guidance for health and pharma innovations
Mentorship from BIRAC's expert review committee
Linkage to BIRAC's international partner programs (UK, US, Israel, Singapore)
grants.howToApply: Apply online through the BIRAC portal during an open window. Submit the innovation concept, host institution letter, and PI details. Shortlisted applicants present to a technical review panel. The entire process takes 3-4 months from application to approval.

grants.title: SBIRI - Small Business Innovation Research Initiative
grants.amountLabel: Up to Rs.1.5 Cr (Phase I) and up to Rs.5 Cr (Phase II)
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual call - typically Q3 each year
grants.description: Two-phase funding scheme for biotech startups progressing from early prototype to pre-commercialisation. Phase I validates feasibility; Phase II funds scale-up and trials. One of India's largest biotech startup grants.
grants.eligibility: Registered Indian company with fewer than 500 employees. Must be working on a biotech innovation - pharma, diagnostics, agri-biotech, or industrial bio. Phase II requires successful Phase I completion or equivalent validation.
whatYouGet: Phase I: Up to Rs.1.5 Cr as grant for proof-of-concept and feasibility (12-18 months)
Phase II: Up to Rs.5 Cr as soft loan for clinical trials, scale-up, and regulatory approvals (2-3 years)
Access to BIRAC's lab infrastructure and testing networks
Support for regulatory submissions (DCGI, FSSAI, etc.)
Connections to pharma industry partners for licensing or co-development
grants.howToApply: Submit an Expression of Interest (EoI) during the annual open call. Shortlisted applicants submit a full proposal. Review involves scientific peer evaluation and a panel interview. Phase I awardees can directly apply for Phase II upon milestone completion.

grants.title: NIDHI PRAYAS - Pre-Incubation Grant
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - through NIDHI host institutions
grants.description: Pre-incubation funding for innovators at the earliest stage - converting ideas into working prototypes. One of few Indian grants that funds before a company is even incorporated.
grants.eligibility: Students, researchers, faculty, or early-stage innovators at the idea or concept stage. Must be hosted at a NIDHI PRAYAS centre. Individual applications accepted - no company incorporation required.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.10L grant for prototyping materials, equipment access, and technical support
Co-working space and lab access at the host NIDHI-PRAYAS centre
Mentorship from DST's network of industry experts and faculty
IP guidance and patent filing support
Pathway to NIDHI incubation and larger DST funding programs
grants.howToApply: Find a NIDHI-PRAYAS centre near you on the DST/NIDHI portal. Apply directly to the centre with your innovation concept. Centers have quarterly selection cycles.

grants.title: TIDE 2.0 - Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs
grants.deadlineLabel: Through partner incubators - rolling basis
grants.description: Equity-free grant for ICT-based startups building in AI, IoT, cybersecurity, blockchain, AR/VR, and accessibility tech. Channeled through MeitY's 51 partner incubators across India.
grants.eligibility: Tech startup working in ICT domains (AI, IoT, cybersecurity, blockchain, assistive tech). Must apply and get incubated at a MeitY TIDE 2.0 partner incubator. DPIIT recognition is a plus but not mandatory.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.75L equity-free grant disbursed in tranches against milestones
Incubation space, legal support, and accounting assistance
Access to NASSCOM, CII, and industry partner networks through MeitY
Government procurement opportunities through GeM and MeitY channels
Mentorship from 200+ empanelled industry mentors
grants.howToApply: Browse TIDE 2.0 partner incubators on the MeitY website and apply to the most relevant one for your domain. After incubation selection, the incubator nominates you for grant funding.

grants.title: SAMRIDH Accelerator
grants.deadlineLabel: Cohort-based - 2 cohorts per year, check portal for dates
grants.description: MeitY's flagship accelerator for software product startups. Combines funding, mentorship, market access, and global exposure. Strong for B2B SaaS and enterprise tech startups looking to scale.
grants.eligibility: Registered Indian software product startup at early-revenue or growth stage. Must apply through a SAMRIDH partner accelerator. Companies in cloud, SaaS, enterprise software, and platform businesses are well-suited.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.40L grant funding (equity-free, milestone-based)
12-week structured acceleration with weekly sessions from CXOs and investors
Access to MeitY's global market programs in US, UK, Singapore, and UAE
Customer connect with 500+ corporates and PSUs in MeitY's network
Demo Day with 100+ investors including Tier 1 VCs
grants.howToApply: Apply through one of the 8 SAMRIDH partner accelerators (iCreate, T-Hub, NSRCEL, etc.). After selection by the partner accelerator, you go through a panel review by MeitY for grant approval.

grants.title: KSUM Seed Fund
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - quarterly review cycles
grants.description: Seed funding from Kerala's nodal startup agency for early-stage startups incubated within the Kerala ecosystem. One of India's more accessible state seed funds with a straightforward application process.
grants.eligibility: Startup registered in Kerala or with a Kerala-based team incubated at a KSUM-recognised incubator. DPIIT recognition preferred. Must be at prototype or MVP stage.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.15L grant for product development, IP costs, and initial market expenses
Access to KSUM's 14 incubation centres across Kerala
Co-working space and lab facilities at partner incubators
Introductions to Kerala-based angel investors and the Kerala angel network
grants.howToApply: Apply online through the KSUM portal. The seed fund application requires a product demo, financial projections, and team details. Selection panels meet quarterly.

grants.title: T-Hub Lab32 Accelerator
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual - applications typically open Q1 (Jan-Feb)
grants.description: Telangana's flagship accelerator operated out of India's largest incubator. Strong corporate connect program with access to 100+ corporates including Microsoft, HDFC, and TSRTC as pilot customers.
grants.eligibility: Startups with a working product and at least some early traction (pilot customers, LOIs, or early revenue). Open to all sectors. India-wide applications accepted.
whatYouGet: Rs.10L-Rs.30L in funding (mix of grant and equity depending on cohort terms)
6-month acceleration program at T-Hub's 2.6 lakh sq ft campus in Hyderabad
Access to T-Hub's 100+ corporate partners for pilots and PoCs
Mentorship from 300+ mentors including CXOs and serial founders
International exposure through T-Hub's US, Israel, and Japan programs
Demo Day access to 150+ investors
grants.howToApply: Apply through the T-Hub website during the Lab32 application window (typically Jan-Feb). Application includes product demo video, pitch deck, and team profile. Shortlisted teams go through 2 rounds of interviews.

grants.title: NASSCOM 10000 Startups
grants.deadlineLabel: Cohort-based - rolling applications throughout the year
grants.description: India's largest tech startup incubation initiative. Primarily valuable for cloud credits (AWS, Azure, GCP worth Rs.15-70L), investor introductions, and access to NASSCOM's enterprise customer network. No equity taken.
grants.eligibility: Early-stage tech startup across India. Must be product-first and technology-led - service companies are not eligible. Less than 3 years old preferred for the incubation track.
whatYouGet: AWS credits worth up to $100,000 plus Azure and GCP credits
Access to NASSCOM's network of 3,000+ IT companies for enterprise sales
Co-working space at NASSCOM's 10KS hubs across 12 cities
Legal, finance, and HR advisory at subsidised rates
Investor introductions to 200+ VCs and angels in NASSCOM's ecosystem
grants.howToApply: Apply online at 10000startups.com. Rolling applications, no fixed window. Application takes 15 minutes. Selected companies get an interview call within 2-4 weeks.

grants.title: AIM Agriculture Innovation Challenge
grants.deadlineLabel: Periodic - check AIM portal for active agriculture cohort
grants.description: AIM's sector-specific challenge grant for startups solving precision farming, cold chain, post-harvest loss, agri-credit, and farm-to-market supply chain problems.
grants.eligibility: Indian startups or MSMEs with a working prototype or pilot in agriculture, food processing, or rural supply chains. Must demonstrate real farmer or supply chain engagement.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.1 Cr non-dilutive grant disbursed in 3 milestones
Pilot opportunities with government agri departments and FPOs
Access to ICAR (Indian Council of Agricultural Research) network
Introductions to agri-focused VCs and impact investors
Mentorship from agricultural scientists, domain experts, and farmer organisations
grants.howToApply: Apply when an Agriculture challenge theme is live on the AIM portal. The application requires a detailed solution write-up, pilot data, and an impact framework.

grants.title: NABVENTURES Agri and Rural Fund
grants.amountLabel: Rs.25L-Rs.5 Cr (equity investment with grant component)
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - quarterly evaluation cycles
grants.description: NABARD's venture fund arm that invests in agritech, fintech for farmers, rural supply chains, and allied sectors. Takes equity but carries a grant component and non-financial support valuable for rural-focused startups.
grants.eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startup with a product or service demonstrably benefiting farmers, FPOs, or rural communities. Early revenue or strong pilot evidence required.
whatYouGet: Rs.25L-Rs.5 Cr in equity funding plus a non-dilutive grant component
Access to NABARD's 50 lakh+ SHG and FPO network for pilots and distribution
Introductions to co-investors including impact VCs and DFIs
Regulatory support for RBI-regulated products in rural finance
grants.howToApply: Submit an Expression of Interest through the NABVENTURES website. Their investment team typically responds within 4-6 weeks.

grants.title: MNRE Clean Energy Startup Support
grants.org: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual call - typically released Q2 (Apr-Jun)
grants.description: Government grant for early-stage startups developing solar energy, energy storage, green hydrogen, or energy efficiency products. Part of MNRE's push to build a domestic cleantech startup ecosystem.
grants.eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startup working in renewable energy or clean technology. Must have a prototype or pilot deployment. Both hardware and software startups are eligible.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.50L non-dilutive grant for prototype development, testing, and pilot deployment
Access to MNRE's testing and certification labs
Priority consideration for MNRE pilot projects and government installations
Introductions to PSU energy companies (NTPC, SECI, IREDA) for partnerships
grants.howToApply: Applications open through the MNRE portal during annual call. Requires technical specifications, prototype evidence, team details, and projected impact.

grants.title: Climate Launchpad India
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual - national applications typically open March-May
grants.description: World's largest cleantech startup competition, operating in 50+ countries. Indian teams compete in national heats and the winner goes to the global finals in Europe. Entirely non-dilutive prize money.
grants.eligibility: Any team with a climate change solution at idea to early prototype stage. No revenue requirement. Open to founders from any background including students.
whatYouGet: National stage: prize plus coaching and workshop access
Global finals winner: 50,000 EUR prize + access to EIT Climate's European investor network
3-day boot camp with cleantech experts before the national competition
Access to EIT Climate's 300+ partner organisations across Europe for pilots
grants.howToApply: Apply online during the national application window. Submit a 3-minute pitch video and a short application form. Selected teams go through a boot camp before the national competition day.

grants.title: WEE Fund - Women Enterprise Enablement
grants.org: SIDBI (Small Industries Development Bank of India)
grants.amountLabel: Rs.10L-Rs.1 Cr (collateral-free loan with partial grant subsidy)
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - through SIDBI branch and partner bank network
grants.description: SIDBI's flagship scheme for women-led businesses providing collateral-free working capital and term loans at subsidised rates. Includes a grant subsidy component that reduces effective loan cost significantly.
grants.eligibility: Startup or MSME with at least 51% women ownership or managed by women. Must be incorporated in India. Minimum 1 year of business operations preferred.
whatYouGet: Collateral-free loan of Rs.10L-Rs.1 Cr at below-market interest rates
Grant subsidy reducing effective cost by 2-4% per annum
Business development support and capacity building workshops
Access to SIDBI's marketplace for women entrepreneurs
grants.howToApply: Approach SIDBI's nearest regional office or apply through a partner bank (UCO Bank, Bank of Baroda, etc.). Bring incorporation documents, audited financials (if available), business plan, and ownership proof. Processing typically takes 4-8 weeks.

grants.title: Goldman Sachs 10,000 Women Initiative
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual cohort - applications typically open Q4 (Oct-Nov)
grants.description: Highly competitive business education program for women entrepreneurs delivered in partnership with IIM Ahmedabad, IIM Bangalore, and ISB. Covers finance, strategy, marketing, and leadership with a global alumni network of 10,000+ women entrepreneurs across 56 countries.
grants.eligibility: Women business owners with at least 2 years of operating history and a viable business generating some revenue. Open to all sectors. Approximately 10% acceptance rate.
whatYouGet: Fully-funded 6-week management program at IIM/ISB (no fees, travel covered)
Access to Goldman Sachs' global network of 10,000+ women entrepreneur alumni
Post-program mentorship and peer learning circles
Introductions to capital providers and impact investors through GS network
Certification from Goldman Sachs and partner IIM/ISB
grants.howToApply: Apply online through the Goldman Sachs 10KW portal during the annual application window. Requires 2 years of financials, a business essay, and 2 references.

grants.title: Echoing Green Fellowship
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual - applications open September, close November
grants.description: One of the most prestigious global fellowships for social entrepreneurs. Provides unrestricted funding plus intensive leadership development over 2 years. Alumni include founders of City Year, Teach For America, and SKS Microfinance.
grants.eligibility: Early-stage social entrepreneur at idea-to-proof-of-concept stage. Must be addressing a systemic social or environmental problem. Open to Indian founders. Extremely competitive - approximately 1% acceptance rate from 4,000+ applications.
whatYouGet: $90,000 in unrestricted funding over 2 years ($45,000/year)
Healthcare coverage for the fellowship period
Intensive leadership development program and coaching
Access to Echoing Green's 900+ fellow alumni network across 60 countries
Visibility with global impact investors, philanthropies, and media
Lifetime membership in the Echoing Green community
grants.howToApply: Apply online at echoinggreen.org during the September-November window. Application requires a 6-page written application. No pitch deck required. Finalists do a video interview.

grants.title: Villgro Social Enterprise Incubation
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - quarterly cohorts, apply any time
grants.description: India's oldest and most active social enterprise incubator. Focuses on healthcare, agriculture, and education innovations serving low-income communities. Portfolio companies have collectively reached 35 million+ beneficiaries.
grants.eligibility: Social enterprise with a product or service targeting BOP populations in India. Early revenue or strong pilot evidence strongly preferred. Both for-profit and not-for-profit structures accepted.
whatYouGet: Rs.5L-Rs.50L in grant funding over the incubation period (typically 12-18 months)
Dedicated Villgro incubation manager as your primary support contact
Access to Villgro's network of 20+ hospitals, NGOs, and government partners for pilots
Introductions to impact investors, CSR funders, and development finance institutions
Eligibility for follow-on investment from Villgro's equity fund
grants.howToApply: Submit an application on the Villgro website with your product description, pilot data, impact metrics, and financials. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.

grants.title: YUKTI 2.0 Innovation Challenge
grants.org: AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education)
grants.amountLabel: Up to Rs.2L prize + incubation support
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual - typically open Nov-Jan each academic year
grants.description: AICTE national innovation challenge exclusively for students enrolled in AICTE-approved institutions. Covers hardware, software, social, and sustainability innovations. Winners get cash prizes and fast-tracked incubation at AICTE-supported centres.
grants.eligibility: Students currently enrolled in any AICTE-approved college or university (UG, PG, or PhD). Teams of 2-5 students. Faculty mentor required. Startup does not need to be incorporated.
whatYouGet: Cash prize up to Rs.2L for winners at national level
Fast-tracked incubation at an AICTE-supported incubator
Access to AICTE IDEA Lab facilities for prototyping
Recognition on YUKTI portal tracked by DPIIT for NIRF rankings
Mentorship from industry experts during the competition
grants.howToApply: Register on the YUKTI portal during the annual open window. Submit your innovation idea, team details, and faculty mentor information. Shortlisted teams present at regional and national levels.

grants.title: Smart India Hackathon (SIH)
grants.amountLabel: Rs.1L per winning team per problem statement
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual - registrations open July-August each year
grants.description: India's largest national hackathon for student innovators. Problem statements are set by real government ministries and PSUs. Winning solutions are often taken forward for actual government deployment. Both software and hardware editions run simultaneously.
grants.eligibility: Teams of 6 students from any recognised Indian college or university. At least one team member must be enrolled full-time. Students from any discipline can apply, not limited to engineering.
whatYouGet: Rs.1L prize money per winning team for each problem statement solved
Direct interaction with the ministry or PSU that set the problem
Opportunity for winning solution to be piloted by the government department
Certificate of participation from Ministry of Education
Winning teams shortlisted for national-level innovation awards
grants.howToApply: Teams register on the SIH portal during the annual window (July-Aug). Select a problem statement from the published list. Submit a solution idea online. Shortlisted teams are invited for a 36-hour hackathon at a nodal centre.

grants.title: Startup India Campus Challenge
grants.amountLabel: Up to Rs.5L for top teams
grants.deadlineLabel: Annual - typically Q3-Q4 of each year
grants.description: DPIIT dedicated program to identify and support the best student-run startups from Indian campuses. Competing startups get DPIIT recognition fast-tracked, direct mentorship, and cash grants. Open to all disciplines, not just tech.
grants.eligibility: Student-founded startup where at least one founder is currently enrolled in a recognised Indian institution (UG, PG, or PhD). Startup should be early-stage with at most seed funding raised.
whatYouGet: Cash prize up to Rs.5L for top-ranked student startups
Fast-tracked DPIIT recognition for Startup India benefits
Direct mentorship from Startup India mentor network
Introductions to incubators and early-stage investors
Platform visibility on Startup India national network
grants.howToApply: Apply through the Startup India portal during the annual Campus Challenge window. Requires startup details, founder enrollment proof, and a pitch deck.

grants.title: PRISM - Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Startups and MSMEs
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - open year-round through DST portal
grants.description: DST grassroots innovation grant for individual innovators and students who have a novel technological solution but lack resources to build a prototype. One of the few grants with no company or institutional requirement - individuals can apply directly.
grants.eligibility: Individual Indian citizens including students, farmers, artisans, and grassroots innovators. No company incorporation required. The innovation must be original and not commercially available. Can apply without institutional affiliation.
whatYouGet: Up to Rs.15L as a grant for prototype development and field trials
Technical guidance from DST network of scientific institutions
IP support including patent filing assistance
Pathway to higher DST funding programs like NIDHI after prototype validation
Recognition from DST which helps with follow-on funding applications
grants.howToApply: Submit an application on the DST PRISM portal with your innovation description, prototype plan, and impact potential. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis by a DST expert panel.

grants.title: IIT Incubator Pre-Seed Grants
grants.deadlineLabel: Rolling - each IIT incubator has independent cycles
grants.description: IIT-linked incubators (SINE at IIT Bombay, FITT at IIT Delhi, IITM-IC at IIT Madras, NSRCEL at IIM Bangalore) offer pre-seed grants for student and alumni founders. Among the most credible early-stage grants available to student entrepreneurs in India.
grants.eligibility: Current students, recent alumni (within 3-5 years of graduation), or faculty of the respective IIT or IIM. Some incubators also accept external student teams with strong tech innovations. Idea or early prototype stage accepted.
whatYouGet: Rs.2L-Rs.25L in pre-seed grant funding (non-dilutive at most incubators)
Physical office and lab space on the IIT campus
Access to world-class research labs and faculty mentors
Legal, accounting, and IP support at subsidised rates
Warm introductions to the IIT alumni investor network
Credibility that significantly helps with follow-on fundraising
grants.howToApply: Apply directly to the incubator at your nearest IIT or IIM. SINE (IIT Bombay), FITT (IIT Delhi), IITM-IC (IIT Madras), and NSRCEL (IIM Bangalore) all have online application portals. Selection involves a written application and a pitch to the incubation committee.
